title: IngestResult.outcome is typed 'written'|'skipped' but ingestImage returns 'written' for dry-run even when no write occurred
description: IngestResult.outcome is declared as `'written' | 'skipped'` (line 41). In `ingestImage` the dry-run path returns `{ outcome: 'written', plan: plan! }` (line 503) even though nothing was written to disk. Callers in cli.ts and ingestBatch use `outcome === 'written'` to increment `result.written` (lines 723, 889), so dry-run always reports written=N instead of 0, violating the BatchResult contract. A third outcome value (`'dry-run'`) or a `dryRun` flag in the result is needed.

title: tiling-ingest tests use imageIdForPath synchronously but it is async, causing always-undefined imageId
description: `imageIdForPath` in `hash.ts` is declared `async` (returns `Promise<string>`). In `tiling-ingest.test.ts` lines 58 and 84, it is called without `await`: `const imageId = imageIdForPath(master)`. The resulting `imageId` is a Promise object, not a string, so the subsequent `join(out, 'images', imageId, 'manifest.json')` constructs a path with `[object Promise]`, causing the readFile to throw with ENOENT and the assertion after it is never reached, masking the real test contract. The test gives a false green when it should fail.

title: tiling-ingest.test.ts expects ingestImage to return the string 'written' but IngestResult is an object
description: `ingestImage` returns `Promise<IngestResult>` (an object with `outcome`, `stagedBytes`, etc.), not a string. Line 56 of the test asserts `expect(await ingestImage(...)).toBe('written')`, which compares an object to the string `'written'` and will always be false, meaning the test silently fails its write-confirmation assertion. The correct assertion is `expect((await ingestImage(...)).outcome).toBe('written')`.

title: planShard returns empty array silently for out-of-range i or n<=0 instead of throwing as the test asserts
description: `planShard` for `i < 0 || i >= n` returns `[]` (line 29) and for `n <= 0` returns `items.slice()` (line 28). The test in `shard.test.ts` (lines 18-20) asserts `expect(() => planShard(['a'], 2, 2)).toThrow()`, `expect(() => planShard(['a'], -1, 2)).toThrow()`, and `expect(() => planShard(['a'], 0, 0)).toThrow()`. The tests are wrong (they pass against the actual no-throw implementation only if the test runner treats no-throw as a test failure internally), OR the tests are contract documentation that the implementation violates. Either the implementation should throw, or the tests need correcting тАФ the contract is internally inconsistent.

title: Tier-5 stub buildFallbackPlan builds an IngestPlan whose manifest.format field accepts values outside MasterFormat union
description: The Tier-5 stub manifest (lines 592-601) sets `format: detected as any` where `detected` comes from `detectFormatByMagic(bytes) || 'unknown'`. The `masterInfoSchema` (schema.ts line 55) includes `'unknown'` as a valid enum value. However `IngestPlan.master.format` is typed as `MasterFormat` which is `'orf' | 'dng' | 'cr2' | 'jpg'` (backends.ts line 5) and does not include `'unknown'`. The `as any` cast at lines 594 and 578 suppresses this mismatch. A caller deserializing a Tier-5 manifest via `parseManifest` gets `'unknown'` back, which cannot be assigned to `MasterFormat` without another cast. The schema and type should be reconciled.

title: readCheckpoint parses checkpoint JSON without schema validation, silently returning malformed state
description: `readCheckpoint` at lines 64-71 reads the checkpoint file and does `JSON.parse(txt) as CheckpointState` with no Zod/runtime validation. A truncated or partially-written checkpoint file (e.g. from a crash after the tmp write but before rename, or from disk corruption) would parse as `{}` or partial JSON. The callers in `ingestBatch` then read `checkpoint.completed`, `checkpoint.failed`, and `checkpoint.inFlight` which would all be `undefined`, causing `.map()` and `.filter()` calls to throw a TypeError at runtime. There is no guard for missing array fields.

title: CliEvent type declares 'image-done' event but cli.ts emits 'image-end', creating a reader/writer mismatch
description: The `CliEvent` union type in `schema.ts` (lines 150-157) declares `{ type: 'image-done'; ... }` as the per-image success event. However, `cli.ts` line 895 emits `tel?.event?.('image-end' | 'image-failed', ...)` and `ingestBatch` at lines 731 and 895 emit `'image-end'`. Any consumer reading the JSON event stream and discriminating on `type === 'image-done'` will never match. The declared contract (`image-done`) does not match what is emitted (`image-end`).

title: migrateSchema with targetVersion=4 writes schema:4 but the raw transform adds no v4-specific fields, producing invalid manifests that may fail validation
description: `SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_TARGETS = [2, 4]` (line 81) allows migration to schema version 4. The transform lambda at line 99 only does `{ ...raw, schema: targetVersion, producedBy: makeProducedBy() }`. However `manifestSchemaV4Base` extends V2 only with `schema: z.literal(4)` тАФ no additional required fields тАФ so round-trip survives Zod. But if future v4 schema fields are added as required, existing data migrated by this bare transform would fail `parseManifest`. More critically, the CLI's `--migrate-schema` path at cli.ts line 328 does `parseInt(parsed['migrate-schema'], 10) as any` without validating against `SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_TARGETS`, meaning `--migrate-schema 3` or `--migrate-schema 99` is only caught inside `migrateSchema` and returns an error record instead of rejecting at the schema level.

title: toEntry sets bytes=level.data.length but after dry-run truncation data becomes Uint8Array(0), recording 0 bytes in the manifest entry
description: `toEntry` computes `bytes: level.data.length` at line 37. In `ingestImage` dry-run mode (ingest.ts lines 498-501), every level's `data` is replaced with `new Uint8Array(0)` before building the plan return value. If `computeIngestPlan` is called and the plan's entries are already built before the data zeroing, the entries will have the correct `bytes`. But the `IngestPlan.levels` array (typed inline, not as LevelEntry[]) passes through to `applyIngestPlan` which calls `toEntry` on those levels тАФ so the zero-byte levels would produce entries with `bytes: 0`. The real path does `toEntry` before zeroing (in `computeIngestPlan`), but the zeroing code at line 500 mutates the levels that are already in `plan.entries` via the `preEntries` pass-through in `writeLevelFiles`. Net: the manifest written by a subsequent non-dry-run invocation is correct, but any display of the dry-run plan (via `printPlanHuman`) will show 0.0 KB for all levels.

title: measureConvergenceProfile skips levels with max(w,h) < 1024 but QualityCurvePoint schema requires bytes > 0 while bytesPushed starts at 0
description: In `measureConvergenceProfile` (backends.ts line 346), the curve point is built as `{ bytes: bytesPushed }` at the moment the `'progress'` event fires. If the decoder fires a `'progress'` event before any chunk has been pushed (which is theoretically possible if the decoder emits a preview from the container header before push completes), `bytesPushed` would be 0. The `qualityCurvePointSchema` at schema.ts line 34 validates `bytes: z.number().int().positive()` тАФ so `bytes: 0` would cause `parseManifest` to throw when the manifest is loaded. This is an edge case that depends on decoder behavior, but the schema guard would silently destroy the manifest of any affected level on next load.

title: CliEvent union type is declared but never validated against actual emission at test or runtime boundaries
description: The `CliEvent` type (schema.ts lines 150-157) documents the JSON event stream contract for `--json` mode consumers. There is no Zod schema for it and no test that captures the stdout events and validates them against the type. The existing test in `cli.test.ts` that checks `--json` output (lines 151-172) only checks `type` strings exist and `imageId`/`runId` presence тАФ it does not verify field shapes, types, or that the emitted event set matches `CliEvent`. A broken field (e.g. the `image-done` vs `image-end` mismatch in contracts-7) is undetectable without a schema-level assertion.

title: manifestSchema discriminated union has no forward-compatibility sentinel for schema versions > 4
description: The discriminated union `manifestSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('schema', [v1, v2base, v4base])` (line 91) hard-fails with a Zod parse error for any `schema` value other than 1, 2, or 4 тАФ including a future schema 5 written by a newer tool version. The `producedBy.version` major-version guard (lines 79-84) protects against forward-incompatible tool versions, but a future tool writing schema 5 would cause `parseManifest` to throw immediately before the `producedBy` check is reached. Adding an explicit 'unrecognised schema' passthrough or a `z.number()` catch-all with graceful degradation would allow older clients to at least surface a useful error instead of a cryptic Zod union failure.

title: ingestBatch has no idempotency guard when called concurrently with same outDir and overlapping file sets
description: Multiple concurrent `ingestBatch` invocations targeting the same `outDir` (e.g. from two CLI processes, or two test runs) acquire the gallery-level write lock for the batch, but per-image write locks are acquired only when processing each image. If two workers from different batches race on the same image, the second acquires the image lock after the first releases it and re-ingests the file (wasting work). There is no stable batch-scope idempotency key: the `batchId` UUID in checkpoint is per-invocation and is not visible across process boundaries. Adding a deterministic per-image idempotency key (e.g. `imageId + mtimeMs`) surfaced in the manifest would allow concurrent orchestrators to detect and skip duplicate work without relying on the per-image lock.
